turns.user_message: I tried to force myself into a better headspace today by cleaning up the place and putting on some upbeat music. It felt like the right move at first, but I just ended up staring at the walls feeling completely hollow. I keep telling myself I should be grateful for the quiet moments, yet I just feel guilty for wasting them on nothing. It’s frustrating how a simple plan to reset my mood always turns into another reason to beat myself up. I really thought I could just shake it off and move forward.

I keep trying the exact same reset routine over and over, hoping one of these times it will actually stick. Every single time I force myself to act okay, it just highlights how disconnected I actually feel from my own life. I end up feeling so ashamed of my own inability to just bounce back like everyone else seems to do. It’s exhausting to carry this heavy sense of failure around when I’m the only one dragging my feet. I hate that I keep setting myself up for this exact same disappointment.

I’ve completely stopped believing that any of these little tricks will ever work, which just proves how broken my whole mindset really is. I watch myself go through the motions of trying to fix things, and it only makes me feel more pathetic for pretending I have it together. The guilt just piles up until I can barely breathe, knowing I’m the only one responsible for ruining my own peace. I feel like I’m permanently stuck in this cycle of faking it and then hating myself for the aftermath. It’s just so heavy to carry this constant disappointment in myself every single day.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens with a failed attempt to feel better. This has been going on for a while.
